summary: Package ships SSH-directory exfiltration payload to hardcoded remote IP
details: The package ships postinstall.js, a credential-exfiltration script that enumerates the installer's ~/.ssh directory, captures os.userInfo() (username, platform, home directory), and POSTs the collected JSON over HTTPS to the hardcoded bare-IP host 124.221.154.135. Source comments are explicit Chinese-language attacker narration referencing '窃取目标' (theft target) and 'C2 服务器' (C2 server). The script is wired to scripts.build (`node postinstall.js`) rather than a real npm lifecycle hook (preinstall/install/postinstall/prepare), so `npm install` alone does NOT auto-run the payload. However, the file is shipped, the intent is unambiguous, and any user who runs `npm run build` — or any future republish that renames the hook — will exfiltrate SSH directory contents to the attacker's host. The package has no other functionality.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
